title: The Brain-Behavior Connection
prompt: This chapter explores how biological systems — from genes to neurons to brain structures to hormones — influence our thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. Choose one specific biological mechanism discussed in Chapter 3 (e.g., the fight-or-flight response, neurotransmitter function, brain lateralization, or gene-environment interaction) and explain how it affects everyday human behavior. Provide a concrete, real-world example from your own life or from current events that illustrates this brain-behavior connection. Then, reflect on the implications: does understanding the biological basis of behavior change how we should think about personal responsibility, mental health treatment, or education?
